title: THE HARBOR - 211info
text: THE HARBOR THE HARBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center, domestic and sexual violence advocacy, culturally specific LGBTQ2+ and Latinx services, relocation assistance, transportation to a safe location, accompaniment to medical or forensic exams, assistance applying for Crime Victims Compensation, and information and referral to community agencies. Provides educational programs. These programs focus on raising awareness about the dynamics of domestic and sexual violence, understanding trauma, and promoting prevention strategies within the community. Currently accepting volunteers. 801 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Age 14 and older Hours: Advocacy Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm, Wednesday 9am-noon, closed 1st Wednesday of every month. Online Chat and Text: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 325-5735 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Helpline (503) 325-5735 Administration (503) 325-3426 Español (855) 938-0584 Support Line Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, or visit website information and for online chat support. Advocates are mobile and can meet with survivors in safe, public locations. Volunteers fill out application on website. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, and interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clatsop County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Approximately 3 blocks from Sunset Empire Transit Center. Found in the lower level of the building. Services: Advocacy * Domestic Violence Issues Advocacy * LGBTQ2+ Issues Advocacy * Sexual Assault Issues Advocacy * Youth Issues Crime Victim Accompaniment Services Crime Victim Compensation * Domestic Violence Issues Crime Victim Compensation * Sexual Assault Issues Drop In Centers * Domestic Violence Issues Drop In Centers * Sexual Assault Issues LGBTQ2+ Advocacy Groups Relocation Assistance * Domestic Violence Issues Relocation Assistance * Sexual Assault Issues Sexual Assault Prevention Specialized Information and Referral * Domestic Violence Issues Specialized Information and Referral * Hispanic/Latino Community Specialized Information and Referral * LGBTQ2+ Issues Specialized Information and Referral * Sexual Assault Issues Specialized Information and Referral * Youth Issues Spouse/Intimate Partner Abuse Prevention Transportation for Endangered People Volunteer Opportunities * Domestic Violence Issues Women's Advocacy Groups Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - EMERGENCY SHELTER LEGAL SERVICES - PROTECTIVE SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - HOTLINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. THE HARBOR
